overall_notes: Microsoft's 2025 Environmental Sustainability Report (covering FY2024) is a comprehensive and detailed disclosure with strong evidence across carbon, water, waste, and ecosystem dimensions. Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions are quantified with multi-year trend data and independently reviewed by Deloitte & Touche LLP, and the net-zero/carbon-negative commitment is underpinned by specific 2030 milestones, a named decarbonisation pathway, and board-level governance. The principal gap is the absence of SBTi-validated or SBTi-committed targets for Microsoft's own operations, which is a notable omission given the maturity of the rest of the disclosure.

scores.dimension_name: Net Zero commitment and transition plan
scores.evidence_quote: By 2030, Microsoft will cut its emissions by more than half compared to 2020 and remove more carbon than it emits.
scores.scoring_rationale: Microsoft states a specific carbon-negative target by 2030 with quantified interim milestones (>50% emission reduction from 2020 baseline, 100% CFE by 2025), a named decarbonisation pathway (CFE procurement, carbon removal, supplier engagement), and board-level governance via the Environmental, Social, and Public Policy Committee and the internal Climate Council with cross-company representation.

scores.evidence_quote: Our Scope 1 and 2 emissions decreased by 30% from the 2020 base year... driven by our ongoing work to advance clean energy procurement.
scores.scoring_rationale: Quantified Scope 1 and Scope 2 (market-based) emissions are disclosed with year-on-year comparison across FY20–FY24 (Carbon Table 1, p.12), a 29.9%/30% reduction from the 2020 baseline is reported, and Deloitte & Touche LLP performed a review of specified information in the Environmental Data Fact Sheet, constituting third-party assurance.

scores.evidence_quote: Microsoft's Scope 3 emissions continue to account for more than 97% of our total emissions... increased by 26% from our 2020 baseline.
scores.scoring_rationale: Scope 3 emissions are disclosed across 11 named GHG Protocol categories with percentage breakdowns and absolute figures (Carbon Table 2, p.13), a methodology note references management's criteria and the GHG Protocol, and Deloitte & Touche LLP's review of the Environmental Data Fact Sheet covers this data.

scores.evidence_quote: Deloitte & Touche LLP performed a review relating to specified information within Section 1 of the Environmental Data Fact Sheet.
scores.scoring_rationale: A named third party (Deloitte & Touche LLP) performed external assurance of GHG and environmental data; however, the engagement is described as a 'review' of 'specified information' rather than full reasonable assurance to a named standard such as ISAE 3000, and the assurance statement itself is published in the separate Data Fact Sheet rather than within this report, limiting the score to 2.

scores.evidence_quote: streamlined decision-making and alignment to science-based targets
scores.evidence_location: p.59 (Microsoft for Sustainability — Envirotainer case study)
scores.scoring_rationale: The document does not disclose that Microsoft itself has signed an SBTi commitment letter, submitted targets for SBTi validation, or received SBTi-validated targets; the sole reference to 'science-based targets' appears in a customer case study, not in relation to Microsoft's own targets, so only an aspirational/indirect signal is present at best.
